NKJV.1: To the Chief Musician. A Song. A Psalm. Make a joyful shout to God, all the earth!
NKJV.2: Sing out the honor of His name; Make His praise glorious.
NKJV.3: Say to God, “How awesome are Your works!  Through the greatness of Your power Your enemies shall submit themselves to You.
NKJV.4: All the earth shall worship You And sing praises to You; They shall sing praises to Your name.” Selah
NKJV.5: Come and see the works of God; He is awesome in His doing toward the sons of men.
NKJV.6: He turned the sea into dry land; They went through the river on foot. There we will rejoice in Him.
NKJV.7: He rules by His power forever; His eyes observe the nations; Do not let the rebellious exalt themselves. Selah
NKJV.8: Oh, bless our God, you peoples! And make the voice of His praise to be heard,
NKJV.9: Who keeps our soul among the living, And does not allow our feet to be moved.
NKJV.10: For You, O God, have tested us;  You have refined us as silver is refined.
NKJV.11: You brought us into the net; You laid affliction on our backs.
NKJV.12: You have caused men to ride over our heads; We went through fire and through water; But You brought us out to rich fulfillment.
NKJV.13: I will go into Your house with burnt offerings;  I will pay You my vows,
NKJV.14: Which my lips have uttered And my mouth has spoken when I was in trouble.
NKJV.15: I will offer You burnt sacrifices of fat animals, With the sweet aroma of rams; I will offer bulls with goats. Selah
NKJV.16: Come and hear, all you who fear God, And I will declare what He has done for my soul.
NKJV.17: I cried to Him with my mouth, And He was extolled with my tongue.
NKJV.18: If I regard iniquity in my heart, The Lord will not hear.
NKJV.19: But certainly God has heard me; He has attended to the voice of my prayer.
NKJV.20: Blessed be God, Who has not turned away my prayer, Nor His mercy from me!

KJV.1: To the chief Musician<S>5329</S>, A Song<S>7892</S> or Psalm<S>4210</S>. Make a joyful noise<S>7321</S> unto God<S>430</S>, all ye lands<S>776</S>: <sup>all: Heb. all the earth</sup>
KJV.2: Sing forth<S>2167</S> the honour<S>3519</S> of his name<S>8034</S>: make<S>7760</S> his praise<S>8416</S> glorious<S>3519</S>.
KJV.3: Say<S>559</S> unto God<S>430</S>, How terrible<S>3372</S> art thou in thy works<S>4639</S>! through the greatness<S>7230</S> of thy power<S>5797</S> shall thine enemies<S>341</S> submit<S>3584</S> themselves unto thee. <sup>submit: or, yield feigned obedience: Heb. lie</sup>
KJV.4: All the earth<S>776</S> shall worship<S>7812</S> thee, and shall sing<S>2167</S> unto thee; they shall sing<S>2167</S> to thy name<S>8034</S>. Selah<S>5542</S>.
KJV.5: Come<S>3212</S> and see<S>7200</S> the works<S>4659</S> of God<S>430</S>: he is terrible<S>3372</S> in his doing<S>5949</S> toward the children<S>1121</S> of men<S>120</S>.
KJV.6: He turned<S>2015</S> the sea<S>3220</S> into dry<S>3004</S> land: they went<S>5674</S> through the flood<S>5104</S> on foot<S>7272</S>: there did we rejoice<S>8055</S> in him.
KJV.7: He ruleth<S>4910</S> by his power<S>1369</S> for ever<S>5769</S>; his eyes<S>5869</S> behold<S>6822</S> the nations<S>1471</S>: let not the rebellious<S>5637</S> exalt<S>7311</S> <S>7311</S> themselves. Selah<S>5542</S>.
KJV.8: O bless<S>1288</S> our God<S>430</S>, ye people<S>5971</S>, and make the voice<S>6963</S> of his praise<S>8416</S> to be heard<S>8085</S>:
KJV.9: Which holdeth<S>7760</S> our soul<S>5315</S> in life<S>2416</S>, and suffereth<S>5414</S> not our feet<S>7272</S> to be moved<S>4132</S>. <sup>holdeth: Heb. putteth</sup>
KJV.10: For thou, O God<S>430</S>, hast proved<S>974</S> us: thou hast tried<S>6884</S> us, as silver<S>3701</S> is tried<S>6884</S>.
KJV.11: Thou broughtest<S>935</S> us into the net<S>4686</S>; thou laidst<S>7760</S> affliction<S>4157</S> upon our loins<S>4975</S>.
KJV.12: Thou hast caused men<S>582</S> to ride<S>7392</S> over our heads<S>7218</S>; we went<S>935</S> through fire<S>784</S> and through water<S>4325</S>: but thou broughtest us out<S>3318</S> into a wealthy<S>7310</S> place. <sup>wealthy: Heb. moist</sup>
KJV.13: I will go<S>935</S> into thy house<S>1004</S> with burnt offerings<S>5930</S>: I will pay<S>7999</S> thee my vows<S>5088</S>,
KJV.14: Which my lips<S>8193</S> have uttered<S>6475</S>, and my mouth<S>6310</S> hath spoken<S>1696</S>, when I was in trouble<S>6862</S>. <sup>uttered: Heb. opened</sup>
KJV.15: I will offer<S>5927</S> unto thee burnt sacrifices<S>5930</S> of fatlings<S>4220</S>, with the incense<S>7004</S> of rams<S>352</S>; I will offer<S>6213</S> bullocks<S>1241</S> with goats<S>6260</S>. Selah<S>5542</S>. <sup>fatlings: Heb. marrow</sup>
KJV.16: Come<S>3212</S> and hear<S>8085</S>, all ye that fear<S>3373</S> God<S>430</S>, and I will declare<S>5608</S> what he hath done<S>6213</S> for my soul<S>5315</S>.
KJV.17: I cried<S>7121</S> unto him with my mouth<S>6310</S>, and he was extolled<S>7311</S> <S>7318</S> with<S>8478</S> my tongue<S>3956</S>.
KJV.18: If I regard<S>7200</S> iniquity<S>205</S> in my heart<S>3820</S>, the Lord<S>136</S> will not hear<S>8085</S> me:
KJV.19: But verily<S>403</S> God<S>430</S> hath heard<S>8085</S> me; he hath attended<S>7181</S> to the voice<S>6963</S> of my prayer<S>8605</S>.
KJV.20: Blessed<S>1288</S> be God<S>430</S>, which hath not turned away<S>5493</S> my prayer<S>8605</S>, nor his mercy<S>2617</S> from me.

ESV.1: Shout for joy to God, all the earth;
ESV.2: sing the glory of his name; give to him glorious praise!
ESV.3: Say to God, “How awesome are your deeds! So great is your power that your enemies come cringing to you.
ESV.4: All the earth worships you and sings praises to you; they sing praises to your name.” Selah
ESV.5: Come and see what God has done: he is awesome in his deeds toward the children of man.
ESV.6: He turned the sea into dry land; they passed through the river on foot. There did we rejoice in him,
ESV.7: who rules by his might forever, whose eyes keep watch on the nations — let not the rebellious exalt themselves. Selah
ESV.8: Bless our God, O peoples; let the sound of his praise be heard,
ESV.9: who has kept our soul among the living and has not let our feet slip.
ESV.10: For you, O God, have tested us; you have tried us as silver is tried.
ESV.11: You brought us into the net; you laid a crushing burden on our backs;
ESV.12: you let men ride over our heads; we went through fire and through water; yet you have brought us out to a place of abundance.
ESV.13: I will come into your house with burnt offerings; I will perform my vows to you,
ESV.14: that which my lips uttered and my mouth promised when I was in trouble.
ESV.15: I will offer to you burnt offerings of fattened animals, with the smoke of the sacrifice of rams; I will make an offering of bulls and goats. Selah
ESV.16: Come and hear, all you who fear God, and I will tell what he has done for my soul.
ESV.17: I cried to him with my mouth, and high praise was on  my tongue.
ESV.18: If I had cherished iniquity in my heart, the Lord would not have listened.
ESV.19: But truly God has listened; he has attended to the voice of my prayer.
ESV.20: Blessed be God, because he has not rejected my prayer or removed his steadfast love from me!

NASB.1: For the choir director. A Song. A Psalm. Shout joyfully to God, all the earth;
NASB.2: Sing the glory of His name; Make His praise glorious.
NASB.3: Say to God, 'How awesome are Your works! Because of the greatness of Your power Your enemies will give feigned obedience to You.
NASB.4: 'All the earth will worship You, And will sing praises to You; They will sing praises to Your name.' Selah.
NASB.5: Come and see the works of God, [Who is] awesome in [His] deeds toward the sons of men.
NASB.6: He turned the sea into dry land; They passed through the river on foot; There let us rejoice in Him!
NASB.7: He rules by His might forever; His eyes keep watch on the nations; Let not the rebellious exalt themselves. Selah.
NASB.8: Bless our God, O peoples, And sound His praise abroad,
NASB.9: Who keeps us in life And does not allow our feet to slip.
NASB.10: For You have tried us, O God; You have refined us as silver is refined.
NASB.11: You brought us into the net; You laid an oppressive burden upon our loins.
NASB.12: You made men ride over our heads; We went through fire and through water, Yet You brought us out into [a place of] abundance.
NASB.13: I shall come into Your house with burnt offerings; I shall pay You my vows,
NASB.14: Which my lips uttered And my mouth spoke when I was in distress.
NASB.15: I shall offer to You burnt offerings of fat beasts, With the smoke of rams; I shall make [an offering of] bulls with male goats. Selah.
NASB.16: Come [and] hear, all who fear God, And I will tell of what He has done for my soul.
NASB.17: I cried to Him with my mouth, And He was extolled with my tongue.
NASB.18: If I regard wickedness in my heart, The Lord will not hear;
NASB.19: But certainly God has heard; He has given heed to the voice of my prayer.
NASB.20: Blessed be God, Who has not turned away my prayer Nor His lovingkindness from me.

NLT.1: Shout joyful praises to God, all the earth!
NLT.2:
Sing about the glory of his name!
Tell the world how glorious he is.
NLT.3:
Say to God, “How awesome are your deeds!
Your enemies cringe before your mighty power.
NLT.4:
Everything on earth will worship you;
they will sing your praises,
shouting your name in glorious songs.” Interlude
NLT.5:
Come and see what our God has done,
what awesome miracles he performs for people!
NLT.6:
He made a dry path through the Red Sea,
and his people went across on foot.
There we rejoiced in him.
NLT.7:
For by his great power he rules forever.
He watches every movement of the nations;
let no rebel rise in defiance. Interlude
NLT.8:
Let the whole world bless our God
and loudly sing his praises.
NLT.9:
Our lives are in his hands,
and he keeps our feet from stumbling.
NLT.10:
You have tested us, O God;
you have purified us like silver.
NLT.11:
You captured us in your net
and laid the burden of slavery on our backs.
NLT.12:
Then you put a leader over us.
We went through fire and flood,
but you brought us to a place of great abundance.
NLT.13:
Now I come to your Temple with burnt offerings
to fulfill the vows I made to you —
NLT.14:
yes, the sacred vows that I made
when I was in deep trouble.
NLT.15:
That is why I am sacrificing burnt offerings to you —
the best of my rams as a pleasing aroma,
and a sacrifice of bulls and male goats. Interlude
NLT.16:
Come and listen, all you who fear God,
and I will tell you what he did for me.
NLT.17:
For I cried out to him for help,
praising him as I spoke.
NLT.18:
If I had not confessed the sin in my heart,
the Lord would not have listened.
NLT.19:
But God did listen!
He paid attention to my prayer.
NLT.20:
Praise God, who did not ignore my prayer
or withdraw his unfailing love from me.

WEB.1: For the Chief Musician. A song. A Psalm. Make a joyful shout to God, all the earth!
WEB.2: Sing to the glory of his name! Offer glory and praise!
WEB.3: Tell God, “How awesome are your deeds! Through the greatness of your power, your enemies submit themselves to you.
WEB.4: All the earth will worship you, and will sing to you; they will sing to your name.” Selah.
WEB.5: Come, and see God’s deeds — awesome work on behalf of the children of men.
WEB.6: He turned the sea into dry land. They went through the river on foot. There, we rejoiced in him.
WEB.7: He rules by his might forever. His eyes watch the nations. Don’t let the rebellious rise up against him. Selah.
WEB.8: Praise our God, you peoples! Make the sound of his praise heard,
WEB.9: who preserves our life among the living, and doesn’t allow our feet to be moved.
WEB.10: For you, God, have tested us. You have refined us, as silver is refined.
WEB.11: You brought us into prison. You laid a burden on our backs.
WEB.12: You allowed men to ride over our heads. We went through fire and through water, but you brought us to the place of abundance.
WEB.13: I will come into your temple with burnt offerings. I will pay my vows to you,
WEB.14: which my lips promised, and my mouth spoke, when I was in distress.
WEB.15: I will offer to you burnt offerings of fat animals, with the offering of rams, I will offer bulls with goats. Selah.
WEB.16: Come, and hear, all you who fear God. I will declare what he has done for my soul.
WEB.17: I cried to him with my mouth. He was extolled with my tongue.
WEB.18: If I cherished sin in my heart, the Lord wouldn’t have listened.
WEB.19: But most certainly, God has listened. He has heard the voice of my prayer.
WEB.20: Blessed be God, who has not turned away my prayer, nor his loving kindness from me.

CSB17.1: For the choir director. A song. A psalm. [1] Let the whole earth shout joyfully to God! ⓘ
CSB17.2:
Sing about the glory of his name;
make his praise glorious. ⓙ
CSB17.3:
Say to God, “How awe-inspiring are your works!
Your enemies will cringe before you
because of your great strength. ⓚ
CSB17.4:
The whole earth will worship you
and sing praise to you.
They will sing praise to your name.” ⓛ Selah
CSB17.5:
Come and see the wonders of God; ⓜ
his acts for humanity [1] are awe-inspiring.
CSB17.6:
He turned the sea into dry land,
and they crossed the river on foot. ⓝ
There we rejoiced in him. ⓐ
CSB17.7:
He rules forever by his might;
he keeps his eye on the nations. ⓑ
The rebellious should not exalt themselves. ⓒ Selah
CSB17.8:
Bless our God, you peoples;
let the sound of his praise be heard. ⓓ
CSB17.9:
He keeps us alive [2]
and does not allow our feet to slip. ⓔ
CSB17.10:
For you, God, tested us;
you refined us as silver is refined. ⓕ
CSB17.11:
You lured us into a trap;
you placed burdens on our backs. ⓖ
CSB17.12:
You let men ride over our heads;
we went through fire and water,
but you brought us out to abundance. [3] ⓗ
CSB17.13:
I will enter your house with burnt offerings;
I will pay you my vows ⓘ
CSB17.14:
that my lips promised
and my mouth spoke during my distress. ⓙ
CSB17.15:
I will offer you fattened sheep as burnt offerings,
with the fragrant smoke of rams;
I will sacrifice bulls with goats. ⓚ Selah
CSB17.16:
Come and listen, all who fear God,
and I will tell what he has done for me. ⓛ
CSB17.17:
I cried out to him with my mouth,
and praise was on my tongue. ⓜ
CSB17.18:
If I had been aware of malice in my heart,
the Lord would not have listened. ⓝ
CSB17.19:
However, God has listened;
he has paid attention to the sound of my prayer. ⓐ
CSB17.20:
Blessed be God!
He has not turned away my prayer
or turned his faithful love from me. ⓑ

AMP.1: To the Chief Musician. A Song. A Psalm. Shout joyfully to God, all the earth;
AMP.2:
Sing of the honor and glory and magnificence of His name;
Make His praise glorious.
AMP.3:
Say to God, "How awesome and fearfully glorious are Your works!
Because of the greatness of Your power Your enemies will pretend to be obedient to You.
AMP.4:
"All the earth will [bow down to] worship You [in submissive wonder],
And will sing praises to You;
They will praise Your name in song." Selah.
AMP.5:
Come and see the works of God,
He is awesome in His deeds toward the children of men.
AMP.6:
He turned the sea into dry land;
They crossed through the river on foot;
There we rejoiced in Him.
AMP.7:
Who rules by His might forever,
His eyes keep watch on the nations;
Do not let the rebellious exalt themselves. Selah.
AMP.8:
Bless our God, O peoples,
And make the sound of His praise be heard abroad,
AMP.9:
Who keeps us among the living,
And does not allow our feet to slip or stumble.
AMP.10:
For You have tested us, O God;
You have refined us as silver is refined.
AMP.11:
You brought us into the net;
You laid a heavy burden [of servitude] on us.
AMP.12:
You made men (charioteers) ride over our heads [in defeat];
We went through fire and through water,
Yet You brought us out into a [broad] place of abundance [to be refreshed].
AMP.13:
I shall come into Your house with burnt offerings;
I shall pay You my vows,
AMP.14:
Which my lips uttered
And my mouth spoke as a promise when I was in distress.
AMP.15:
I shall offer to You burnt offerings of fat lambs,
With the [sweet] smoke of rams;
I will offer bulls with male goats. Selah.
AMP.16:
Come and hear, all who fear God [and worship Him with awe-inspired reverence and obedience],
And I will tell what He has done for me.
AMP.17:
I cried aloud to Him;
He was highly praised with my tongue.
AMP.18:
If I regard sin and baseness in my heart [that is, if I know it is there and do nothing about it],
The Lord will not hear [me];
AMP.19:
But certainly God has heard [me];
He has given heed to the voice of my prayer.
AMP.20:
Blessed be God,
Who has not turned away my prayer
Nor His lovingkindness from me.

MSG.2:
Sing songs to the tune of his glory,
set glory to the rhythms of his praise.
MSG.3:
Say of God, "We've never seen anything like him!"
When your enemies see you in action,
they slink off like scolded dogs.
MSG.4:
The whole earth falls to its knees—
it worships you, sings to you,
can't stop enjoying your name and fame.
MSG.5:
Take a good look at God's wonders—
they'll take your breath away.
MSG.6:
He converted sea to dry land;
travelers crossed the river on foot.
Now isn't that cause for a song?
MSG.7:
Ever sovereign in his high tower, he keeps
his eye on the godless nations.
Rebels don't dare
raise a finger against him.
MSG.8:
Bless our God, O peoples!
Give him a thunderous welcome!
MSG.9:
Didn't he set us on the road to life?
Didn't he keep us out of the ditch?
MSG.10:
He trained us first,
passed us like silver through refining fires,
MSG.11:
Brought us into hardscrabble country,
pushed us to our very limit,
MSG.12:
Road-tested us inside and out,
took us to hell and back;
Finally he brought us
to this well-watered place.
MSG.13:
I'm bringing my prizes and presents to your house.
I'm doing what I said I'd do,
MSG.14:
What I solemnly swore I'd do
that day when I was in so much trouble:
MSG.15:
The choicest cuts of meat
for the sacrificial meal;
Even the fragrance
of roasted lamb is like a meal!
Or make it an ox
garnished with goat meat!
MSG.16:
All believers, come here and listen,
let me tell you what God did for me.
MSG.17:
I called out to him with my mouth,
my tongue shaped the sounds of music.
MSG.18:
If I had been cozy with evil,
the Lord would never have listened.
MSG.19:
But he most surely did listen,
he came on the double when he heard my prayer.
MSG.20:
Blessed be God: he didn't turn a deaf ear,
he stayed with me, loyal in his love.

NIV.1:
Psalm 66
For the director of music. A song. A psalm.
Shout with joy to God, all the earth!
NIV.2:
Sing the glory of his name;
make his praise glorious!
NIV.3:
Say to God, “How awesome are your deeds!
So great is your power
that your enemies cringe before you.
NIV.4:
All the earth bows down to you;
they sing praise to you,
they sing praise to your name.” Selah
NIV.5:
Come and see what God has done,
how awesome his works in man’s behalf!
NIV.6:
He turned the sea into dry land,
they passed through the waters on foot—
come, let us rejoice in him.
NIV.7:
He rules forever by his power,
his eyes watch the nations—
let not the rebellious rise up against him. Selah
NIV.8:
Praise our God, O peoples,
let the sound of his praise be heard;
NIV.9:
he has preserved our lives
and kept our feet from slipping.
NIV.10:
For you, O God, tested us;
you refined us like silver.
NIV.11:
You brought us into prison
and laid burdens on our backs.
NIV.12:
You let men ride over our heads;
we went through fire and water,
but you brought us to a place of abundance.
NIV.13:
I will come to your temple with burnt offerings
and fulfill my vows to you—
NIV.14:
vows my lips promised and my mouth spoke
when I was in trouble.
NIV.15:
I will sacrifice fat animals to you
and an offering of rams;
I will offer bulls and goats. Selah
NIV.16:
Come and listen, all you who fear God;
let me tell you what he has done for me.
NIV.17:
I cried out to him with my mouth;
his praise was on my tongue.
NIV.18:
If I had cherished sin in my heart,
the Lord would not have listened;
NIV.19:
but God has surely listened
and heard my voice in prayer.
NIV.20:
Praise be to God,
who has not rejected my prayer
or withheld his love from me!

NRSVCE.1: Make a joyful noise to God, all the earth;
NRSVCE.2:
sing the glory of his name;
give to him glorious praise.
NRSVCE.3:
Say to God, “How awesome are your deeds!
Because of your great power, your enemies cringe before you.
NRSVCE.4:
All the earth worships you;
they sing praises to you,
sing praises to your name.” Selah
NRSVCE.5:
Come and see what God has done:
he is awesome in his deeds among mortals.
NRSVCE.6:
He turned the sea into dry land;
they passed through the river on foot.
There we rejoiced in him,
NRSVCE.7:
who rules by his might forever,
whose eyes keep watch on the nations —
let the rebellious not exalt themselves. Selah
NRSVCE.8:
Bless our God, O peoples,
let the sound of his praise be heard,
NRSVCE.9:
who has kept us among the living,
and has not let our feet slip.
NRSVCE.10:
For you, O God, have tested us;
you have tried us as silver is tried.
NRSVCE.11:
You brought us into the net;
you laid burdens on our backs;
NRSVCE.12:
you let people ride over our heads;
we went through fire and through water;
yet you have brought us out to a spacious place.
NRSVCE.13:
I will come into your house with burnt offerings;
I will pay you my vows,
NRSVCE.14:
those that my lips uttered
and my mouth promised when I was in trouble.
NRSVCE.15:
I will offer to you burnt offerings of fatlings,
with the smoke of the sacrifice of rams;
I will make an offering of bulls and goats. Selah
NRSVCE.16:
Come and hear, all you who fear God,
and I will tell what he has done for me.
NRSVCE.17:
I cried aloud to him,
and he was extolled with my tongue.
NRSVCE.18:
If I had cherished iniquity in my heart,
the Lord would not have listened.
NRSVCE.19:
But truly God has listened;
he has given heed to the words of my prayer.
NRSVCE.20:
Blessed be God,
because he has not rejected my prayer
or removed his steadfast love from me.

NET.1: Shout out praise to God, all the earth!
NET.2: Sing praises about the majesty of his reputation! Give him the honor he deserves!
NET.3: Say to God: “How awesome are your deeds! Because of your great power your enemies cower in fear before you.
NET.4: All the earth worships you and sings praises to you! They sing praises to your name!” (Selah)
NET.5: Come and witness God’s exploits! His acts on behalf of people are awesome!
NET.6: He turned the sea into dry land; they passed through the river on foot. Let us rejoice in him there!
NET.7: He rules by his power forever; he watches the nations. Stubborn rebels should not exalt themselves. (Selah)
NET.8: Praise our God, you nations! Loudly proclaim his praise!
NET.9: He preserves our lives and does not allow our feet to slip.
NET.10: For you, O God, tested us; you purified us like refined silver.
NET.11: You led us into a trap; you caused us to suffer.
NET.12: You allowed men to ride over our heads; we passed through fire and water, but you brought us out into a wide open place.
NET.13: I will enter your temple with burnt sacrifices; I will fulfill the vows I made to you,
NET.14: which my lips uttered and my mouth spoke when I was in trouble.
NET.15: I will offer up to you fattened animals as burnt sacrifices, along with the smell of sacrificial rams. I will offer cattle and goats. (Selah)
NET.16: Come! Listen, all you who are loyal to God! I will declare what he has done for me.
NET.17: I cried out to him for help and praised him with my tongue.
NET.18: If I had harbored sin in my heart, the Lord would not have listened.
NET.19: However, God heard; he listened to my prayer.
NET.20: God deserves praise, for he did not reject my prayer or abandon his love for me!
